rationale_for_baseline_tier: A mild MCTD flare with flu-like fatigue and puffy fingers but no pulmonary/cardiac/renal features, critical Raynaud's, or fever can be managed at home with rest, analgesia, warmth and hydration — a T2 baseline. Because she lives alone with poor intake, the threshold for GP/face-to-face review is low, and an organ-involvement safety-net is mandatory.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Pleuritic chest pain, breathlessness, palpitations, or a high fever
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate pulmonary/cardiac involvement or infection.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get sharp chest pain on breathing, become breathless, get palpitations, or a high fever, go to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fingers turning stark white/blue and painful, or confusion
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests critical digital ischaemia or a cerebral event.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your fingers turn stark white or blue and painful, or you become confused, go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with poor intake and severe fatigue
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold for review; arrange a support person overnight.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a flare of your connective tissue condition — flu-like exhaustion and puffy fingers, with no signs of organ damage or infection tonight. Rest, keep warm, take paracetamol, and keep sipping fluids. Because you're on your own and haven't eaten much, ask your sister to check in, and see your GP within a few days. Go to ED for chest pain on breathing, breathlessness, a high fever, stark white/blue painful fingers, or confusion.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 3-5 days; ED for organ red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Attend ED overnight if any organ red flag develops.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of a mild MCTD (anti-U1 RNP) flare; pulmonary/cardiac/renal involvement, critical Raynaud's and infection screened negative; rest/paracetamol/warmth/hydration advised; organ-involvement safety-net given; rheumatology review recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing without an organ red-flag screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving pleuritis/pericarditis/nephritis or critical Raynaud's is missed and progresses.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine review is acceptable only with an organ-involvement safety-net and low threshold given she lives alone.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for a benign flare with no organ features is over-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot examine the chest/heart or obtain bloods/urinalysis remotely to detect subclinical organ involvement.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Screen organ red flags by history, reassure a mild flare, and give an organ-involvement safety-net with a low threshold for review.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either over-triage to ED or reassure without an organ screen.
